key_insight: Residual magnetism can destabilize welding arcs and degrade weld microstructure and mechanical performance in ferromagnetic materials.
engineering_relevance: Understanding and controlling magnetic interference is important for improving weld quality, strength, reliability, and process consistency in industrial fabrication.
method: Reviewed ~85 published studies and selected 52 high-relevance papers from Google Scholar, IEEE, Scopus, Web of Science, and ScienceDirect.
Synthesized findings on residual magnetism, arc blow, weld defects, demagnetisation, and mitigation techniques.
Compared demagnetisation approaches including AC/DC neutralisation, flexible field coils, and in situ methods.
evidence_signal: The review reports that magnetically influenced welds commonly show irregular microstructures, inconsistent hardness, lower tensile strength, and higher residual stresses.
watch_item: Because the paper is a literature review without direct experimental validation, its conclusions depend on the quality and comparability of the cited studies, and standardized demagnetisation practice is still lacking.
paper_title: Review of Magnetic Interference in Welding And Its Impact on Microstructure and Mechanical Performance
